name: Normalization vs Query Complexity
description: Experience the query complexity of a deeply normalized schema by flattening it into views and a denormalized table

checks.name: v_sales_by_country returns 3 countries
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM v_sales_by_country;
checks.description: The view should return one row per country (Brazil, Argentina, USA)

checks.name: USA has the highest total sales
checks.description: v_sales_by_country should be ordered by total_sales descending; USA (2300) is highest

checks.name: Alice is rank 1 in Brazil
checks.query: SELECT rank_in_country FROM v_top_customers_by_country WHERE customer_name = 'Alice';
checks.description: Alice has the most sales in Brazil (1400 total), so rank_in_country = 1

checks.name: dim_customer_flat has 5 rows with country populated
checks.query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS n FROM dim_customer_flat WHERE country_name IS NOT NULL;
checks.description: All 5 customers should be present with country_name filled in
